 {"id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","title":"Verification Gate: performance optimization (profiling + regression guards)","description":"## Purpose

Verify all performance optimization sub-beads have met their acceptance criteria with profiling evidence, behavioral equivalence proofs, and regression guards.

## Scope

1. All profiling baselines recorded and reproducible
2. Each optimization has before/after criterion comparison
3. All behavioral equivalence golden tests passing
4. No performance regressions in unrelated benchmarks
5. Structured logging for all new metrics
6. E2E script running full benchmark suite with pass/fail thresholds

## Acceptance criteria
- All sub-bead E2E scripts pass
- All sub-bead unit tests pass
- Criterion regression detection shows no regressions
- Flamegraph evidence for all optimized paths
- Summary report: operation, before, after, improvement %","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:54.704845253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:33.719075944Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","performance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:18:54.704845253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:29.163604407Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:30.256388988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:31.455563445Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:32.539409732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:33.718120302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.10","title":"Program Gate: V1.2 end-to-end acceptance (all epics + cross-cutting validation)","description":"## Purpose

Final V1.2 program acceptance gate. Validates all 9 epic verification gates have passed and cross-cutting concerns are operationally sound.

## Scope

### Scenarios

1. All 9 epic verification gate E2E scripts exist and pass
2. Workspace builds clean (fmt + clippy + 0 warnings)
3. Workspace tests pass (all crates, 0 failures)
4. Performance optimization gate (Epic 1) — profiling baselines and regression guards
5. FUSE writeback-cache gate (Epic 2) — correctness proofs and performance gains
6. Safe-merge gate (Epic 3) — correctness under high contention
7. Adaptive repair gate (Epic 4) — expected-loss improvement
8. Multi-host repair gate (Epic 5) — distributed repair correctness
9. btrfs expansion gate (Epic 6) — multi-device and subvolume correctness
10. xfstests gate (Epic 7) — conformance baseline with regression detection
11. Observability gate (Epic 8) — metrics, TUI, evidence presets
12. Test coverage gate (Epic 9) — test count targets met
13. CLI ergonomics and error messaging (all new commands)
14. Structured logging traceability across all new surfaces

### E2E script

`scripts/e2e/ffs_v12_program_gate_e2e.sh` — 14 scenarios, each producing `SCENARIO_RESULT` markers.

## Acceptance criteria
- 14/14 scenarios PASS
- All epic verification gates pass
- Workspace clean
- RELEASE RECOMMENDATION: PROCEED","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:14.984393625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:58.727785714Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:14.984393625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:49.223564252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:50.395471520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:51.564604738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:52.799485483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:53.959347624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:55.112597761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:56.286694803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:57.465102576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.10","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:58.726633224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.2","title":"EPIC: FUSE Writeback-Cache Support with MVCC Boundary Correctness","description":"## Purpose

Enable FUSE writeback-cache mode for dramatically improved write performance. Currently disabled (V1.1 OQ4 resolution: explicit flush vs fsync contract with writeback-cache disabled). Requires proving MVCC boundary correctness under reordered daemon delivery.

## Why

The FUSE writeback cache can batch and reorder writes before delivering them to the filesystem, which can break MVCC commit ordering assumptions. V1.1 conservatively disabled it. V1.2 should enable it with correctness proofs.

## Scope

### Sub-beads

1. **Design: writeback-cache MVCC interaction model** — Formal analysis of write reordering effects on MVCC snapshot visibility, commit ordering, and crash consistency. Define the invariants that must hold. Expected-loss decision matrix for enabling writeback-cache.

2. **Implement writeback-cache commit barrier** — The mechanism that ensures MVCC commit boundaries are respected despite FUSE daemon write reordering. Likely: epoch-based commit barriers, deferred visibility until flush/fsync confirmation.

3. **Crash consistency under writeback-cache** — Prove that crash at any point during writeback-cached operation recovers to a consistent state. Crash matrix with writeback-cache enabled.

4. **Performance validation** — Measure write throughput improvement with writeback-cache enabled. Target: >2x improvement on small-write workloads.

5. **Verification gate** — Correctness proofs, crash matrix, performance comparison, E2E validation.

## Non-goals
- Read caching changes (already handled by kernel page cache)
- Changing existing non-writeback-cache behavior","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.038010152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.038010152Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","design","fuse","mvcc","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.038010152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-m5wf.2.1","title":"Design writeback-cache MVCC interaction model and invariants","description":"## Purpose

Formally analyze how FUSE writeback-cache write reordering interacts with MVCC snapshot visibility and commit ordering. Define invariants.

## Scope

1. **Write reordering model** — Document all ways FUSE daemon can reorder writes when writeback-cache is enabled: batching, merging, delayed delivery, out-of-order delivery.

2. **MVCC invariant analysis** — For each reordering scenario, prove whether snapshot isolation is maintained. Identify which reorderings are safe and which violate MVCC semantics.

3. **Commit barrier design** — Design the mechanism (epoch barriers, flush synchronization, deferred visibility) that preserves MVCC correctness under writeback-cache.

4. **Expected-loss decision matrix** — Quantify: probability of data loss under each design option × severity. Select the design with lowest expected loss.

5. **Design document** — docs/design-writeback-cache-mvcc.md with formal invariants, proofs, and decision matrix.

## Acceptance criteria
- Design document with formal invariants
- Expected-loss decision matrix
- All reordering scenarios enumerated and analyzed
- Commit barrier mechanism specified
- Unit tests for invariant checker","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.872006500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:10:09.579655327Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","design","fuse","mvcc","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:35.872006500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-m5wf.2.2","title":"Implement writeback-cache commit barrier mechanism","description":"## Purpose

Implement the commit barrier mechanism that preserves MVCC ordering under FUSE writeback-cache reordering.

## Scope

1. **Epoch-based commit barriers** in ffs-fuse — Mark MVCC commit boundaries as epoch transitions. Writes within an epoch can be reordered freely; epoch boundaries are flush points.

2. **Deferred visibility** — Writes delivered out of order are not visible to other snapshots until the epoch commits.

3. **Flush synchronization** — fsync/fsyncdir triggers epoch commit, making all buffered writes visible and durable.

4. **Configuration** — Feature-gated: `--writeback-cache` CLI flag enables the mode. Default remains disabled for safety.

## Acceptance criteria
- Commit barrier implementation with epoch tracking
- Deferred visibility for out-of-order writes
- Flush triggers epoch commit
- CLI flag for opt-in enablement
- Unit tests: epoch transitions, deferred visibility, flush semantics
- E2E script: writeback-cache mode mount, write, fsync, verify","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:36.711193200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:58.695079046Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","fuse","mvcc","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:36.711193200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:58.694174110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.2.3","title":"Prove crash consistency under writeback-cache operation","description":"## Purpose

Prove that crash at any point during writeback-cached operation recovers to a consistent MVCC state.

## Scope

1. **Crash matrix** — 10+ crash points covering:
   - Crash during buffered write (before epoch commit)
   - Crash during epoch flush
   - Crash after fsync but before writeback-cache drain
   - Crash during concurrent epochs
   - Crash with partially-committed epoch

2. **Recovery verification** — After each crash point: WAL replay, verify MVCC state consistency, verify no partial epochs visible, verify fsync'd data durable.

3. **Comparison** — Same crash matrix with writeback-cache disabled, verify identical recovery outcomes.

## Acceptance criteria
- 10+ crash point scenarios
- Recovery verification for each crash point
- No partial epoch data visible after crash
- fsync'd data survives all crash points
- E2E crash matrix script with structured logging
- Unit tests for crash recovery logic","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:37.582917859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:59.767777648Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","fuse","mvcc","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:37.582917859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:27:59.766789526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.2.4","title":"Measure writeback-cache performance improvement","description":"## Purpose

Quantify the write throughput improvement from enabling FUSE writeback-cache.

## Scope

1. **Benchmark workloads** — Compare writeback-cache enabled vs disabled:
   - 1000 small writes (4KB each)
   - Sequential write (100MB)
   - Random write (10000 × 4KB)
   - Mixed read+write (80/20)

2. **Latency distribution** — p50/p95/p99 write latency for each workload.

3. **Criterion integration** — Benchmarks as criterion groups for regression detection.

## Acceptance criteria
- Before/after throughput comparison for 4 workloads
- >2x improvement target for small-write workload
- Latency distribution recorded
- Criterion benchmarks integrated
- E2E script with structured reporting","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:38.471291986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:10:16.640715738Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarking","fuse","performance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:19:38.471291986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:00.818604764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
